description: African American labor organizer (1913-1997)
description_wiki: Angelo Braxton Herndon (May 6, 1913 – December 9, 1997) was an African-American labor organizer arrested and convicted of insurrection after attempting to organize black and white industrial workers in 1932 in Atlanta, Georgia.

description: African-American educator and political activist (1868-1936)
description_wiki: John Hope (June 2, 1868 – February 22, 1936), born in Augusta, Georgia, was an American educator and political activist, the first African-descended president of both Morehouse College in 1906 and of Atlanta University in 1929, where he worked to develop graduate programs.

description: American photographer and photojournalist (1903-1975)
description_wiki: Walker Evans (November 3, 1903 – April 10, 1975) was an American photographer and photojournalist best known for his work for the Resettlement Administration and the Farm Security Administration (FSA) documenting the effects of the Great Depression.
